findings.legacy_pattern: (stores.canonical as { importSnapshot?: (entity: Entity) => Promise<Entity> }).importSnapshot; if (typeof canonicalImport !== 'function') { ... }
findings.expected_pattern: await stores.canonical.importSnapshot(entity); // direct call — contract now guarantees it
findings.evidence: src/ops/backup.ts L113-116 repeats at L138-141 (artifact.importSnapshot), L175-178 (ledger.importEvent), L195-198 (ledger.importReceipt). Contracts now require these methods (STORES-R2-002) — optional-cast type assertions and typeof !== 'function' guards are dead-code AND undermine the type-level guarantee.

findings.legacy_pattern: if (resolved.store === 'artifact') { object = sanitizeArtifactForOutput(...); } else if (resolved.store === 'canonical') { ... }  // index/ledger/receipt left raw
findings.expected_pattern: Mirror SDK.resolve 5-arm coverage — also sanitize index (sanitizeIndexRecordForOutput), ledger (sanitizeProvenanceEventForOutput), and receipt (sanitizeReceiptForOutput).
findings.evidence: src/mcp/server.ts L540-545 covers only 2 of 5 store types. SDK.resolve (cluster-sdk.ts L234-244) covers all 5 when policyEnforced; in no-policy path returns raw (L247). MCP handler is the boundary backstop but covers only 2/5.
findings.abstraction: MCP cluster_resolve output sanitization across all 5 store types

findings.legacy_pattern: provenanceEvents: filteredProvenance  // NOT routed through redactProvenanceEvent
findings.expected_pattern: provenanceEvents: bundleRules.length > 0 ? filteredProvenance.map((ev) => redactProvenanceEvent(ev, bundleRules)) : filteredProvenance
findings.evidence: src/policy/redactor.ts L114-118 (wave-edited JSDoc) names: 'Redact a single ProvenanceEvent surfaced through traceProvenance, retrieveBundle.provenanceEvents'. policy-enforced-kernel.ts L378-391 applies bundleRules to entities + artifacts but provenanceEvents only go through path-based filtering — NO call to redactProvenanceEvent.

findings.expected_pattern: tmp + rename atomic pattern matching ingest() L107-122 with cleanup on failure
findings.evidence: L150-164 importSnapshot uses plain writeFileSync — the EXACT pattern Wave A3 just replaced in ingest() (STORES-R2-005). Sibling helper, same write semantics, not migrated.

findings.legacy_pattern: const decision = this.enforce('read_owner_truth', { ownerStore, resourceUri }); const entity = await this.kernel.inspectEntity(id);  // PolicyDeniedError vs NotFoundError discriminates existence per-resource
findings.expected_pattern: Apply the inspectCommand double-enforce pattern (KERNEL-R2-001): coarse pre-fetch gate WITHOUT resource specifics, then fetch, then refine.
findings.evidence: src/kernel/policy-enforced-kernel.ts L139-144 inspectEntity has the EXACT existence-oracle shape that inspectCommand (L470-500) was just fixed for. Per-resource policy denials → enumerable existence via error-type difference.
findings.abstraction: Existence-oracle fix for per-resource enforce+fetch surfaces (KERNEL-R2-001)

findings.legacy_pattern: if (resolved.store === 'artifact') ... else if 'canonical' ... else if 'receipt' ...  // index/ledger NOT sanitized
findings.expected_pattern: Add `else if 'index'` and `else if 'ledger'` arms to mirror SDK.resolve 5-arm coverage.
findings.evidence: src/cli.ts L643-649 covers only 3 of 5 store types. Wave's own comment L605-612 promises 'baseline path safe'. No-policy CLI invocation `db-cluster resolve cluster://index/<id>` prints raw IndexRecord.metadata.
findings.abstraction: CLI resolve baseline sanitization across all 5 store types (SURFACE-R2-001/R2-003)

findings.legacy_pattern: doctor() has no check that consumes mutation_orphaned events — a cluster with orphans still reports healthy.
findings.expected_pattern: Mirror src/ops/verify.ts L154-189: query ledger for action='mutation_orphaned' and emit a HealthCheck with status='degraded' if any exist.
findings.evidence: src/kernel/cluster-kernel.ts L322-329 (wave-edited comment) claims: 'doctor() / verify() can flag it'. verify.ts L150-189 has the check; doctor.ts (210 lines) has zero matches for mutation_orphaned. Promised consumer, missing migration.
findings.abstraction: mutation_orphaned signal consumption across health surfaces (STORES-R2-003)

findings.legacy_pattern: rule matches only `switch (...) { $$$ }` patterns — if/else chains slip through
findings.expected_pattern: Add patterns for if/else if chains on resolved.store and parsed.store OR have completeness-checks.mjs do follow-up text inspection.
findings.evidence: src/mcp/server.ts L541-545 and src/cli.ts L643-647 both use if/else chains covering only 2-3 of 5 store types — V1-002 and V1-006 above are exactly the patterns R4 was meant to catch.
findings.abstraction: R4 completeness rule coverage of store-discriminator switches

findings.legacy_pattern: files: - 'src/contracts/*.ts'  // ONLY contracts. backup.ts call-site `{ importSnapshot?: ... }` casts invisible.
findings.expected_pattern: Add sibling rule scanning src/ops/, src/adapters/, src/sdk/ for the optional-cast call-site patterns.
findings.evidence: src/ops/backup.ts L113/L138/L175/L195 retain optional-cast pattern; R5 doesn't see them because it only scans contracts.
findings.abstraction: R5 completeness rule coverage of optional-method legacy patterns across all source dirs

findings.legacy_pattern: switch on resolved.store covers 5 cases, no default exhaustiveness guard
findings.expected_pattern: Add `default: const _exhaustive: never = resolved.store; ...` so a 6th store type added to ResolvedObject's union surfaces as TS error.
findings.evidence: L233-247 covers 5 current types but lacks exhaustiveness. A 6th store type added to ResolvedObject would skip every case and fall through L247 returning RAW owner truth — latent SURFACE-R2-003 regression.

findings.legacy_pattern: result[key] = rule.behavior === 'strip' ? undefined : REDACTED;  // strip emits undefined (falsy / indistinguishable from missing field)
findings.expected_pattern: result[key] = REDACTED;  // unify strip and mask behaviors as in the just-fixed redactProvenanceEvent
findings.evidence: L209-217 redactMetadataActors and L185-207 redactProvenanceActors still carry the asymmetric strip-vs-mask shape that the wave's KERNEL-R2-006 fix at L171-179 unified for redactProvenanceEvent. Sibling graph-level helpers not harmonized.
findings.abstraction: Actor-redaction semantics harmonization between flat-event and graph-level redactors (KERNEL-R2-006)

findings.legacy_pattern: { rebuilt, clearedFirst: true }  // claims clear-first, but performIndexRebuild now uses atomic replaceAll
findings.expected_pattern: { rebuilt, atomic: true }  // reflect KERNEL-R2-003 semantics
findings.evidence: rebuildIndex L892-899 provenance detail still asserts clearedFirst:true; performIndexRebuild (L824-871) no longer calls clear(). Auditors reading the ledger see a claim contradicting on-disk semantics.
findings.abstraction: rebuildIndex provenance metadata consistency with performIndexRebuild atomic semantics

findings.legacy_pattern: REQUIRED_STORE_CASES.filter((label) => !text.includes(`'${label}'`) ...);  // text containment, not case-arm detection
findings.expected_pattern: Parse the switch body for actual `case '<store>':` tokens.
findings.evidence: L74-86 postProcessSwitchCompleteness uses substring containment. A switch with comment mentioning all 5 labels but only 1 actual case arm would textually pass.

abstractions_audited: Existence-oracle pattern for resource-bound enforce+fetch surfaces (KERNEL-R2-001)
Switch on resolved.store / parsed.store / *.store across all surfaces
summary: Contract-completeness meta-pattern recurred in Wave A3 at multiple abstraction layers. Top recurrences: (a) backup.ts retains Pick<...> casts + typeof !== 'function' guards at 4 call sites despite STORES-R2-002 contract promotion; (b) LocalArtifactStore.importSnapshot still uses plain writeFileSync while sibling ingest() was migrated to atomic tmp+rename; (c) SDK.resolve sanitization is complete (5/5) but MCP cluster_resolve (2/5) and CLI resolve baseline (3/5) cover only subsets; (d) inspectCommand existence-oracle fix (KERNEL-R2-001) wasn't applied to sibling inspectEntity with identical structure; (e) retrieveBundle.provenanceEvents surface named in wave-edited JSDoc was NOT migrated to use redactProvenanceEvent; (f) doctor() does not consume mutation_orphaned signal that verify() now consumes; (g) new R4 and R5 completeness gates have coverage gaps (R4 misses if/else chains, R5 misses backup.ts call sites).
